d2.solidity_versions.description: The Solidity compiler version range the tool supports. Extract when the paper states which Solidity versions the tool works with, e.g. '0.4.x to 0.8.x'.

d3.multifile_support.description: Whether the tool can analyze projects with multiple Solidity source files (imports). 'yes' if it handles inter-file imports, 'partial' if limited, 'no' if single-file only.

d3.external_calls_support.description: Whether the tool can analyze external contract calls (cross-contract interactions). 'yes' if fully supported, 'partial' if limited, 'no' if ignored.

d3.multicontract_support.description: Whether the tool can analyze multiple contracts in a single analysis session. 'yes' if fully supported, 'partial' if limited, 'no' if single-contract only.

d4.custom_rules.description: Whether users can define custom detection rules. 'full' = extensible rule system (e.g. plugins, custom detectors), 'limited' = some configurability, 'none' = no user-defined rules (tool uses fixed or auto-learned rules only). If the paper says 'no manual pre-defined rules' or 'learns automatically', this is 'none'.

d6.outputs.text.description: Whether the tool can output results as plain text to the terminal/console.

d6.outputs.json.description: Whether the tool can output results in JSON format.

d6.outputs.sarif.description: Whether the tool can output results in SARIF format (Static Analysis Results Interchange Format).

d6.outputs.pdf.description: Whether the tool can output a PDF report of its findings.

d6.outputs.code_locate.description: Whether the tool can pinpoint the exact source code location (file, line, statement) of detected vulnerabilities. true if the tool reports defective source code statements or line numbers.

d6.outputs.explanation.description: Whether the tool provides explanations or repair suggestions for detected vulnerabilities. true if it outputs descriptions, remediation advice, or repair methods.

d7.input.sol.description: Whether the tool accepts Solidity source code (.sol files) as input. true if the paper says it analyzes source code or Solidity files.

d7.input.bytecode.description: Whether the tool accepts EVM bytecode as input. true if the paper says it analyzes bytecode, creation bytecode, or deployed bytecode.

d7.input.runtime.description: Whether the tool accepts EVM runtime bytecode as input (as opposed to creation bytecode). true if it specifically mentions runtime bytecode analysis.

d8.mode.description: The primary vulnerability detection technique. 'static' = pattern matching or AST/IR analysis without execution; 'symbolic' = symbolic execution or constraint solving; 'fuzz' = fuzzing or dynamic testing; 'formal' = formal verification or theorem proving; 'hybrid' = combines multiple techniques (e.g. ML + symbolic, static + dynamic). Choose the SINGLE best fit for the tool's main approach.

d9.activity.stars.description: GitHub star count for the tool's repository. Only extract if the paper explicitly states the number of GitHub stars.

d9.activity.last_update_days.description: Days since the tool's last update/commit. Only extract if explicitly stated.

uses_analysis_technique.description: A specific analysis technique the tool employs, e.g. 'symbolic execution', 'abstract interpretation', 'taint analysis', 'machine learning', 'fuzzing'.

analysis_method.description: A description of the tool's analysis methodology or algorithmic approach, e.g. 'constructs CFG and performs depth-first path traversal'.

analyzes_representation.description: What code representation the tool analyzes, e.g. 'source code AST', 'EVM bytecode', 'opcode sequences', 'control flow graph'.

study_used_version.description: The specific version of the tool used in the paper's evaluation, e.g. 'v0.8.0', 'commit abc123'.

version_used_in_study.description: Same as study_used_version — the version of the tool used in this study's experiments.

vulnerability_type_coverage.description: The types of vulnerabilities the tool can detect, e.g. 'reentrancy, integer overflow, access control'.

analysis_time_seconds_per_contract.description: Average analysis time per contract in seconds as reported in the evaluation.

memory_resource_demand.description: Memory usage or resource requirements reported for the tool, e.g. '81.3MB per contract'.

detected_vulnerabilities_count.description: Total number of vulnerabilities detected in the paper's evaluation dataset.

evaluation_approach.description: How the paper evaluated the tool, e.g. 'benchmark on a large real-world contract corpus' or 'comparison with several baselines on the paper's benchmark dataset'.

detects_issue_type.description: A specific vulnerability or issue type the tool detects, e.g. 'reentrancy', 'integer overflow', 'tx-origin'.

d5.strength.reentrancy.description: The qualitative strength label (weak/medium/strong) for tool capability on reentrancy.

category_ranking.reentrancy.stronger_tha.description: Named tools that the subject tool is stronger than on reentrancy. Use only when the paper explicitly supports the comparison.

d5.strength.access_control.description: The qualitative strength label (weak/medium/strong) for tool capability on access_control.

category_ranking.access_control.stronger.description: Named tools that the subject tool is stronger than on access_control. Use only when the paper explicitly supports the comparison.

d5.strength.arithmetic.description: The qualitative strength label (weak/medium/strong) for tool capability on arithmetic.

category_ranking.arithmetic.stronger_tha.description: Named tools that the subject tool is stronger than on arithmetic. Use only when the paper explicitly supports the comparison.

d5.strength.unchecked_low_level_calls.description: The qualitative strength label (weak/medium/strong) for tool capability on unchecked_low_level_calls.

category_ranking.unchecked_low_level_cal.description: Named tools that the subject tool is stronger than on unchecked_low_level_calls. Use only when the paper explicitly supports the comparison.

d5.strength.denial_of_service.description: The qualitative strength label (weak/medium/strong) for tool capability on denial_of_service.

category_ranking.denial_of_service.stron.description: Named tools that the subject tool is stronger than on denial_of_service. Use only when the paper explicitly supports the comparison.

d5.strength.bad_randomness.description: The qualitative strength label (weak/medium/strong) for tool capability on bad_randomness.

category_ranking.bad_randomness.stronger.description: Named tools that the subject tool is stronger than on bad_randomness. Use only when the paper explicitly supports the comparison.

d5.strength.front_running.description: The qualitative strength label (weak/medium/strong) for tool capability on front_running.

category_ranking.front_running.stronger_.description: Named tools that the subject tool is stronger than on front_running. Use only when the paper explicitly supports the comparison.

d5.strength.time_manipulation.description: The qualitative strength label (weak/medium/strong) for tool capability on time_manipulation.

category_ranking.time_manipulation.stron.description: Named tools that the subject tool is stronger than on time_manipulation. Use only when the paper explicitly supports the comparison.

d5.strength.short_addresses.description: The qualitative strength label (weak/medium/strong) for tool capability on short_addresses.

category_ranking.short_addresses.stronge.description: Named tools that the subject tool is stronger than on short_addresses. Use only when the paper explicitly supports the comparison.

d5.strength.unknown_unknowns.description: The qualitative strength label (weak/medium/strong) for tool capability on unknown_unknowns.

category_ranking.unknown_unknowns.strong.description: Named tools that the subject tool is stronger than on unknown_unknowns. Use only when the paper explicitly supports the comparison.
